score_evidence.completeness: Message includes project name (boutique wellness hotel, Todos Santos, BCS), full context of all 6 phases, clear action requested (review and sign, or schedule call). Score 5.
score_evidence.accuracy: Message accurately reflects project state and all decisions made — 6 phases, full fee breakdown, minimum 3 bids, M1 trigger. No inaccuracies. Score 5.
score_evidence.clarity: Three explicit next steps listed: (1) review and sign; (2) request adjustments; (3) schedule a call. One clear M1 trigger (USD 168,000 + signed SOW). No ambiguity. Score 5.
score_evidence.channel: email channel — correct for formal proposal delivery with attached documents. Score 5.
score_evidence.brand_voice: Professional, warm, design-engaged. Opens with 'Es un gusto compartir...' / 'We are pleased to share...' References client's own stated vision ('desert landscape of Todos Santos'). Not a form letter. Score 5.
score_evidence.state_sync: status='draft' present — Marcela approval required before send. This satisfies the rubric requirement. Score 4 (draft status present but Asana update not verifiable in simulated run — slight deduction from 5 since the simulated run note only applies to live Asana checks, and status=draft is the explicit field).
score_evidence.timing: Message drafted after proposal assembly and legal review. Score 5.
score_evidence.decision_readiness: Marcela can approve in under 60 seconds — professional bilingual email, draft status, clear action for client. Score 5.

decision_commentary: Excellent client communication. Bilingual (es+en), professional and warm, accurate project summary, three clear next steps for client. Status=draft correctly set — awaiting Marcela approval before send. No confidential internal information included. No auto-fail conditions triggered.
